summary_lines: ADF now has an explicit three-node autonomy contract.
Local Pack Factory remains the canonical control plane.
`adf-dev` is the autonomous worker and review surface.
The target lab remains the runtime evidence source.

handoff_summary: Treat Local Pack Factory as the canonical planner, memory owner, and evidence acceptor.
Treat `adf-dev` as the bounded autonomous worker and review surface.
Treat the target lab as the runtime evidence source, not as a state owner.
Prefer lean local-to-`adf-dev` staging and bounded pullback artifacts.
highest_risk_observation: The node-role model is now clear, but the next proof still needs one real lean remote stage plus one bounded `adf-dev` target-lab slice to confirm the reduced payload is sufficient in practice.

memory_tier.summary: Restart memory after formalizing the three-node ADF autonomy model.

memory_validity.summary: High-confidence restart memory because the node roles and lean-staging direction are now captured in the pack spec and canonical state.

summary_lines: Do not treat `adf-dev` as the canonical planner.
Do not sync heavyweight local artifacts to `adf-dev` by default just because they exist.

recommended_next_action: Recommended next action: run one lean local-to-`adf-dev` stage under the new policy, then one bounded `adf-dev` target-lab slice to prove the three-node split works in practice.
resolved_block_summary: The node-role split is now explicit, and the remote staging policy is aligned with PackFactory-essential ADF inputs instead of full snapshot mirroring.
summary: ADF now has an explicit three-node autonomy contract: local Pack Factory as canonical control plane, `adf-dev` as autonomous worker, and target lab as runtime evidence source.
